_note: Round-15 follow-up #2: `collect_function_body_stmts` (the helper backing every dispatcher walker's nested-function recursion) only pierced Arrow / FunctionExpression / ParenthesizedExpression / CallExpression (callee + args). Common shapes like `const handlers = { save: () => dispatch('save') }` (object-literal value) hid inner arrow bodies from the typed-slice collector, the dispatched-name walker, the inline-typed-members pass, the rewrite walker, and the `has_*` boolean walkers. Upstream's TS walker visits every child via `ts.forEachChild`; native now mirrors that breadth — object literals, arrays, conditionals, logical/binary ops, sequences, assignments, member access, awaits, new, template literals, tagged templates, TS-cast wrappers all recurse. Inner here puts a typed `createEventDispatcher<{save: {id: number}}>()` inside an object-literal value's arrow body. Pre-fix that typed slice was invisible to `find_dispatcher_event_type_sources`, the events surface stayed at the lax `[evt: string]: CustomEvent<any>` default, and any consumer handler passed silently. Post-fix the slice reaches the surface; Consumer's `(e: CustomEvent<string>) => void` is NOT assignable to `CustomEvent<{id: number}>` and TS2345 fires on `on:save`.
